Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.

end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# World model update:
# - Left panel has colored blocks that can be rearranged by clicking right panel objects
# - Red block toggles between rows 24-29 and rows 30-33
# - Blue block toggles between rows 20-23 and rows 34-37
# - Green square is movable with arrow keys
# - Goal is unclear, but likely involves arranging blocks and/or moving green square

# Let me try moving the green square to different positions
# First, let me check current green square position
seg = current_frame.segmentation
green_pos = None
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        green_pos = n['boundary']
        print(f"Green square at: {green_pos}")
        break

# The green square is at rows 38-39, cols 10-11 (inside gray container)
# Let me try moving it up to the yellow container area
# Yellow container is at rows 20-23, cols 18-23

# First, let me move the green square up
print("Moving green square up...")
# Need to move from rows 38-39 to rows 20-23
# That's about 18 rows up
# But the green square might be blocked by other objects

# Let me try moving up step by step
actions = ['UP'] * 20  # Move up 20 steps
result = action(actions)
print(f"Board changed: {last_action_result.get('board_changed')}")

# Check green square position
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        print(f"Green square now at: {n['boundary']}")
        break

# Check ASCII around green square
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\nLeft panel around green square:")
for i in range(18, 45):
    print(f"  {i}: {ascii_lines[i][6:26]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
